nodes.name: back_translation_augmentation
nodes.description: Represent toxic-comment back-translation as two directional calls to an opaque neural machine translation atom.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Single-pass translation is the atom; back-translation is pipeline orchestration of two passes.
nodes.critic_notes: Model download, tokenizer/model loading, cache writes, and device placement must happen outside the atom.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: The research packet concludes that MarianMT inference is opaque but has a clear deterministic I/O boundary when model state is injected.

nodes.name: translate_text
nodes.description: Translate text between languages using a preloaded MarianMT-style translation backend.

inputs.name: tgt_lang
inputs.constraints: Non-empty target language code different from src_lang.

inputs.name: model_path
inputs.constraints: Identifier or local path for the frozen model supplied by the orchestrator.

inputs.name: model
inputs.constraints: Preloaded translation backend callable accepting text, src_lang, and tgt_lang.

nodes.type_signature: (text: str, src_lang: str, tgt_lang: str, model_path: str | pathlib.Path, model: Callable[[str, str, str], str]) -> str
nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic text-to-text translation through injected frozen model state.
nodes.critic_notes: This atom intentionally does not call from_pretrained or instantiate transformers objects.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: Back-translation composes two translate_text calls, such as en-to-fr followed by fr-to-en.
